name: Cidacos Greenway
name_original: Santa Eulalia Somera desde Préjano — circular
region: Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa
description: This easy 7.2-mile (11.6 km) circular road cycling route from Préjano offers scenic views of the Cidacos River Valley in La Rioja.
region_intro: Road cycling in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa offers diverse natural landscapes within the Sierra Rioja Baja, a Zone of Special Conservation and part of the La Rioja Biosphere Reserve. The region features prominent peaks like Peña Isasa (1,456 meters) and Peñalmonte, providing challenging ascents and descents. The western part is shaped by the Cidacos River valley, offering varied terrain. This area is characterized by its geological richness and a network of routes suitable for road cycling. Best road cycling routes in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa The most popular road cycling route is Valle del Rio Cidacos and ancient town of Turruncun — circular from Arnedillo, a 40.8 miles (65.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 19 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the Cidacos River valley and passes through an ancient town. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Enciso – Arnedillo and thermal baths loop from Arnedillo, a moderate 26.6 miles (42.8 km) path. This route features the thermal waters around Arnedillo and scenic valley views. Local road cyclists also love the Arnedo and Préjano via Cidacos Greenway Loop, a 13.6 miles (21.9 km) trail leading through the Cidacos Greenway, often completed in about 1 hour. Road cycling in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa is defined by mountainous terrain, the Cidacos River valley, and prominent peaks. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.25 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa?
A: There are over 35 road cycling routes in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 600 times.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of road cycling routes in this region?
A: The region offers a diverse range of routes, including 3 easy, 15 moderate, and 20 difficult road cycling trails. This ensures options for cyclists of all abilities, from leisurely rides to challenging ascents.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990105641/valle-del-rio-cidacos-and-ancient-town-of-turruncun-circular-from-arnedillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Rio Cidacos and ancient town of Turruncun — circular from Arnedillo&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 65.7 km loop exploring the Cidacos River valley. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38168111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enciso – Arnedillo and thermal baths loop from Arnedillo&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 42.8 km path.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa often traverse the scenic Cidacos River valley and offer views of prominent peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Isasa&lt;/a&gt;. You can also pass through areas with thermal waters around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1751534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arnedillo&lt;/a&gt; and explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1814079" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly road cycling routes?
A: Yes, there are easy routes suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383493552/arnedo-and-prejano-via-cidacos-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arnedo and Préjano via Cidacos Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 21.9 km trail that follows the Cidacos Greenway, offering a more relaxed cycling experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa?
A: The region's diverse landscapes, from river valleys to mountainous terrain, are enjoyable across different seasons. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for road cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential winter conditions at higher elevations.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in the area?
A: Many routes are accessible from towns like Arnedillo and Arnedo, which typically offer parking facilities. The regional roads like LR-115, LR-382, and LR-123 provide good access points to various cycling routes, and parking can often be found near trailheads or in nearby villages.

Q: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options along the road cycling routes?
A: Towns and villages such as Arnedillo, Arnedo, and Enciso, which are often starting points or pass-through locations for routes, provide various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation. The thermal waters around Arnedillo also offer opportunities for relaxation and services.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Peñas De Arnedillo, Peñalmonte Y Peña Isasa?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.25 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse natural landscapes, the challenging ascents and descents, and the scenic views of the Cidacos valley towns.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with other activities in the region?
A: Absolutely. The region is part of the La Rioja Biosphere Reserve and offers a rich variety of flora and fauna. Cyclists can enjoy wildlife spotting, particularly rupicolous birds of prey, and the area is also popular for hiking and mountaineering, especially around peaks like Peñalmonte and Peña Isasa.

Q: Are there any routes that feature thermal baths?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38168111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enciso – Arnedillo and thermal baths loop from Arnedillo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that specifically highlights the thermal waters around Arnedillo, offering a unique natural attraction along your ride.
tips:
Préjano is a town in the Rioja Baja. It is located on the left bank of the Ruesca River. It was an old mining town, remains that you can see when you pass by. Today its inhabitants support themselves thanks to agriculture and the industry of Arnedo, the closest town that has more life.

You can visit the two most representative churches in Préjano.
The L-382 road runs along the gravel route of the Greenway before disappearing into a short tunnel. There is a nice wooden viewpoint over the valley.
Famous for its Cuevas del Ajedrezado caves, the LR-115 highway passes through Santa Eulalia Somera and Santa Eulalia Bajera with the impressive sheer rock as a guide.

faqs.question: Is the Santa Eulalia Somera from Préjano — circular route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is considered an easy road ride, making it ideal for beginners, families, and cyclists of all fitness levels. The terrain is predominantly well-paved, ensuring a smooth and comfortable experience.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this racebike route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 11.6 kilometers long and typically takes around 33 minutes to complete on a racebike, depending on your pace.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Santa Eulalia Somera from Préjano — circular route?
faqs.answer: You can usually find parking in the town of Préjano, which serves as the starting point for this circular route. Look for available street parking or designated areas within the village.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque views, especially of the Cidacos River Valley. The broader La Rioja area offers mountainous landscapes, vineyards, and cultivated fields, adding to the charm of your ride. The starting point, Préjano, also has historical significance as a former mining town.

faqs.question: Are there any specific points of interest or landmarks on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, along the route, you'll pass through Préjano itself, which has historical mining remnants. You'll also get views from the Cidacos River Valley Viewpoint and pass near the Cuevas del Ajedrezado of Santa Eulalia Somera.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route is enjoyable for cycling throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and conditions, avoiding the peak heat of summer or the colder, potentially wetter days of winter.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route coincide with parts of the Vía Verde de Préjano and the Camino Natural Via Verde del rio Cidacos (also known as Vía Verde del Cidacos or Via verde Cidacos).

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route is on paved roads, it's generally not recommended for dogs to accompany cyclists on racebike routes due to safety concerns with traffic and the pace of cycling. If you do bring a dog, ensure they are on a leash and consider their safety on roads shared with vehicles.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or to pay an entrance fee to cycle this route. It is freely accessible. The area is part of the Valles de Leza, Jubera, Cidacos y Alhama Biosphere Reserve, but general cycling on public roads does not require special permits.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants in Préjano?
faqs.answer: Préjano is a small town, and while options might be limited, you can usually find local establishments for refreshments or a meal before or after your ride. It's a good idea to check opening hours in advance, especially outside of peak tourist seasons.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this racebike route?
faqs.answer: The route is characterized by predominantly well-paved surfaces, including local roads, state roads, and streets. This makes for a smooth and comfortable racebike experience with minimal technical challenges.

waypoints.name: Cuevas del Ajedrezado of Santa Eulalia Somera
